headline: Court Tariff Ruling Puts Costco Valuation And Pricing Power In Focus
summary: U.S. Court of International Trade orders Customs & Border Protection to issue refunds on certain emergency tariffs. Costco Wholesale (NasdaqGS:COST) is among roughly 2,000 companies pursuing tariff refunds following a Supreme Court ruling on presidential emergency tariff powers. The decision has potential implications for Costco's cost base, cash position, and pricing flexibility across imported merchandise. For a membership warehouse retailer like Costco, which relies on high volumes and...

headline: Stocks Slip Before the Open as Bond Yields Climb on Inflation Worries, U.S. Economic Data on Tap
summary: March S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESH26) are down -0.29%, and March Nasdaq 100 E-Mini futures (NQH26) are down -0.40% this morning as Treasury yields advanced for a fourth consecutive day amid worries that surging energy prices from the Middle East conflict could stoke inflation.

headline: Costco (NASDAQ:COST) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q1 CY2026
summary: Membership-only discount retailer Costco (NASDAQ:COST) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 9.2% year on year to $69.6 billion. Its GAAP profit of $4.58 per share was 0.8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Costco Wholesale Corporation Reports Second Quarter and Year-to-Date Operating Results for Fiscal 2026 and February Sales Results
summary: ISSAQUAH, Wash., March 05, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Costco Wholesale Corporation (“Costco” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: COST) today announced its operating results for the second quarter (twelve weeks) and the first 24 weeks of fiscal 2026, ended February 15, 2026. Net sales for the quarter increased 9.1 percent, to $68.24 billion, from $62.53 billion last year. Net sales for the first 24 weeks increased 8.7 percent, to $134.22 billion, from $123.52 billion last year. Comparable sales for the seco

Net sales for the quarter increased 9.1 percent, to $68.24 billion, from $62.53 billion last year.
